medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Muscular involvement commenced cranially and descended in relation to increasing disease duration. The neuromuscular signs were PEO, dysarthria, dysphonia, limb muscle weakness with wasting, absence of Achilles tendon reflexes, and distal vibration sensory loss.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Chronic conditions and prior diagnoses not specified. Surgical history includes bilateral ptosis operation.
medical_history.family_history: First-degree relatives' conditions not specified. Hereditary disease pattern: autosomal dominant progressive external ophthalmoplegia (adPEO) with hypogonadism.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Motor/sensory/cognitive deficits include PEO, dysarthria, dysphonia, limb muscle weakness with wasting, absence of Achilles tendon reflexes, and distal vibration sensory loss. Cardiovascular: Not specified. Other system-specific abnormal findings: Not specified.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Increased in 6 patients (up to 17-fold above normal in one patient)
Increased in 5 patients (up to 13-fold above normal in one patient)
Slightly increased in 5 of 9 clinically affected patients
Normal in all patients from generation IV

other_tests.findings: Myopathic in facial and proximal limb muscles in 9 of 11 cases. Neurogenic involvement suspected in a few tibialis anterior muscles.
other_tests.changes_over_time: Repeated EMG 2 years later showed myopathy in frontalis muscle in one patient.

other_tests.findings: Signs of axonal neuropathy correlated to clinical signs. Absent or abnormally low sural responses in 5 of 10 patients. F-responses reduced in number or absent in peroneal nerves.

Structural abnormalities of mitochondria, ragged-red fibers, and focal cytochrome c oxidase deficiency in advanced cases. No ragged-red fibers or COX-negative fibers in younger patients despite myopathic EMG.
Pronounced structural alterations in mitochondria in older patients. Few abnormal mitochondria in younger patients.
